novel_features.name: Portfolio Dashboard
novel_features.description: One-screen status of every publication you own: subscriber count, paid count, last-published-at, drafts pending, next scheduled. No tab-switching, no CSV exports.
novel_features.rationale: Substack's web UI has no portfolio concept. Aggregating across publications requires a local SQLite store of synced state that no other Substack tool maintains.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent or human owns multiple Substacks (English + German, free + premium tiers), this is the only command that answers 'what is the state of all of them right now'.

novel_features.name: Cross-Publication Twin
novel_features.description: Duplicate a published post into another publication you own as a draft. Preserves paywall markers, sections, and re-uploads images to the target publication's CDN.
novel_features.rationale: Substack's duplicate-post feature is single-publication. Cross-publication content reuse requires sniffing the source HTML, re-uploading images via /api/v1/image against a different subdomain, and POSTing /api/v1/drafts to the target.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Bilingual or multi-tier creators copy-paste between publications today. This collapses the ritual into one command and leaves the target draft ready for translation or pricing-tier adjustment.

novel_features.name: Subscriber Churn Diff
novel_features.description: Diff two SQLite snapshots of your subscriber list: who newly subscribed, who unsubscribed, who upgraded free->paid, who downgraded paid->free, since a chosen window.
novel_features.rationale: Substack reports subscriber count but never the named delta. The diff requires keeping two snapshots locally and joining on email — a SQLite move, not an API call.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents auditing retention want named churn rows, not aggregate counts. Sunday-evening review or weekly automation reads this list and pipes it forward.

novel_features.name: Cross-Sell Candidates
novel_features.description: SQL join across your publications' subscriber lists: emails paid on one publication but free or absent on the others, sorted by paid-publication coverage. The cross-sell list Substack does not ship.
novel_features.rationale: No single API call surfaces this. It requires joining /api/v1/subscriber/list across N publications keyed by email — a cross-source local join.
novel_features.why_it_matters: The most obvious upsell candidates are paying readers on one of your newsletters who don't know your other ones exist. This command surfaces them for a once-a-month email blast.

novel_features.name: Best Posts Cross-Publication
novel_features.description: Rank posts by views, likes, comments, or restacks within a window. Optionally aggregate across every publication you own to find your overall top performer.
novel_features.rationale: Substack ranks within one publication; this CLI ranks across all of yours from cached dashboard stats joined on engagement metrics.
novel_features.why_it_matters: For repurposing decisions, you need the best post across the portfolio, not within one pub. This is the input for Monday-morning content planning.

novel_features.name: Bilingual Pair Tracker
novel_features.description: Record EN<->DE post pairings in a local table. 'posts pair <en> <de>' adds; 'posts pairs --missing' lists posts in one language with no recorded twin in the other.
novel_features.rationale: No upstream concept of paired translations. A local post_pairs table joins to posts on slug; --missing is a one-line outer-join query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Bilingual newsletter owners forget which posts already have a translation. This command answers it offline and feeds the 'posts twin' flow.

novel_features.name: Full-Text Grep
novel_features.description: FTS5 over post bodies + Notes + comments, ranked by bm25, returning snippets and source URLs. Optional scope (posts/notes/comments/all), publication, and since filter.
novel_features.rationale: Substack's site search is shallow and covers only posts. The local cache stores full bodies and an FTS5 index queries them all in milliseconds.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Agents and writers re-citing their own writing need full-archive search across years. Substack cannot do this; this CLI ships it as a one-liner.

novel_features.name: Schedule Board
novel_features.description: ASCII calendar of the next 30 days showing scheduled posts across every publication you own. Multi-pub editorial overview in one screen.
novel_features.rationale: Substack's schedule view is per-publication and web-only. Reading scheduled_at across cached posts/drafts is a local query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Editorial planning across publications needs one calendar. This is the only command that renders it.

narrative.headline: Every Substack feature, plus a local SQLite database, full-text search across years of writing, and the only cross-publication portfolio view that exists.
narrative.value_prop: Manage every Substack you own from one terminal. Sync posts, drafts, notes, comments, and subscribers into a local SQLite store; FTS-search them offline; diff subscriber lists for honest churn reports; spot cross-sell candidates by joining paid and free lists across publications; and twin published posts into a sibling publication as drafts. Built for owners of multiple Substacks — bilingual writers, paid+free tier creators, anyone with more than one newsletter.
narrative.auth_narrative: Substack has no API tokens. The CLI reads your `connect.sid` and `substack.sid` cookies from a logged-in Chrome session: run `auth login --chrome` once, and the cookies are saved to `~/.config/substack-pp-cli/config.toml`. When the session expires (every few weeks), log into Substack in your browser and rerun the command. No password, no OTP, no scraped credentials.

quickstart.comment: Verify the binary, environment, and any saved session
Paste cookies from a logged-in browser if auth login --chrome is not available
Public endpoint — works without auth and confirms the CLI is reaching Substack
Cross-publication snapshot — the command the web UI cannot produce
Named delta of who joined, left, upgraded, downgraded since last sync
FTS5 over years of posts + notes + comments

troubleshoots.fix: Log into Substack in your Chrome browser and rerun 'substack-pp-cli auth login --chrome' to refresh cookies
Substack's scheduled-publish endpoint changed in 2025; use 'drafts publish <id>' for immediate publish and verify the draft state with 'drafts get <id> --json' first

troubleshoots.symptom: Rate-limited with HTTP 429 from list endpoints
troubleshoots.fix: Lower the request rate: pass '--rate-limit 1' globally — Substack's empirical limit is around 2/s
Image uploads must go to the target publication's subdomain. Run 'auth status' to confirm the cookie is valid for that subdomain
Run 'substack-pp-cli sync --full' to refresh — the SQLite cache is updated only on sync

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for substack-pp-cli when an agent or workflow needs to operate across more than one Substack publication, build offline analytics from synced data, or run any command that the web UI does not expose (subscriber churn diffs, cross-publication ranking, cross-sell joins, FTS over years of writing). Single-publication read-only tasks can usually run against postcli or jakub-k-slys/substack-api; everything that requires local state, cross-pub joins, or the portfolio view is what this CLI is for.

recipes.title: Sunday churn review
recipes.explanation: List every named subscriber event of the last week with dotted-path field selection so output stays compact for piping or agent context

recipes.title: Cross-sell candidate list
recipes.explanation: Surface paying readers on one of your publications who are free or absent on the others. Pipe to jq or a spreadsheet for a one-off campaign

recipes.title: Mirror an English post to German pub as draft
recipes.explanation: Preview the twin operation. Drop --dry-run to actually create the draft in the target publication

recipes.title: Find every mention of a topic across years
recipes.explanation: FTS5 search across posts, notes, and comments returning matched snippets with dotted-path field selection so the agent context stays small

recipes.title: Weekly portfolio brief for agents
recipes.explanation: Compact summary for an agent to read once at the top of a session; uses dotted-path --select to drop unnecessary fields
